steps.title: Join two complete clauses
steps.content: She was not alone in her efforts is a complete clause, and the statement about Fernando Orejuela and Stephanie Shonekan is another complete clause. The transition however interrupts the first clause before the second begins.

steps.title: Reject faulty joins
steps.content: B omits needed punctuation, C misuses a colon before however, and D leaves only commas between two complete clauses. The correct punctuation uses a comma with however and a semicolon before the next clause. The answer is A.
